headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair comparison of Super and Folk for people evaluating personal AI agents. Folk fits into the broader automation and assistant landscape, while Super is purpose-built for agents that actually operate computers and reuse a computer-use cache so repeated workflows get cheaper over time. This page situates both products alongside ChatGPT, Gemini, Siri, Grok, and Orchids, using recent reporting and benchmarks to explain why computer-use and cache reuse matter now.
